title: DodagState _lowest_rank not reset on version change
description:
File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:155-163

When _adopt_version() is called to adopt a new DODAG version, _lowest_rank is reset to INFINITE_RANK. This is correct. However, looking at the MaxRankIncrease logic in _admissible():

```python
def _admissible(self, candidate: ParentCandidate) -> bool:
    cost = candidate.path_cost(self.min_hop_rank_increase)
    if cost >= INFINITE_RANK:
        return False
    if self._lowest_rank >= INFINITE_RANK:
        return True  # No bound yet
    return cost <= self._lowest_rank + self.max_rank_increase
```

The _lowest_rank tracks the best rank achieved in the current DODAG version to implement the "max rank increase" bound (RFC 6550 Section 8.2.2.4).

The bug is subtle: if a node achieves rank 300, then _lowest_rank=300. If it loses all parents and becomes UNJOINED, _lowest_rank remains 300. When it rejoins (same version), it can only accept candidates with cost <= 300 + max_rank_increase. But if it should have joined with rank 500 (the only available parent), it would reject this as inadmissible.

This is a "stuck node" scenario. RFC 6550 suggests that nodes should be able to increase rank to rejoin after isolation, up to the max_rank_increase bound from their LOWEST achieved rank, but the implementation might be too restrictive when the only available path is worse than _lowest_rank + max_rank_increase.

The fix may be to reset _lowest_rank when becoming UNJOINED, or to track lowest-this-session vs lowest-ever separately.
close_reason: Reset of _lowest_rank on UNJOINED already in select_parent (prevents stuck node after isolation). Removed explanatory comments per no-comments rule. ruff passes. Commit b20a1b346.
